# offlyn.ai

## Useful intelligence. Fewer tokens. Lower watts. Less cloud water.

Offlyn.ai helps teams reduce AI operating cost and sustainability impact by routing work across local, hybrid, edge, and cloud models based on quality, privacy, cost, carbon, water, and resilience constraints.

---

## The question we help teams answer

> What should run locally, what should run at the edge, and what should go to the cloud?

Cloud AI is powerful, but not every task needs to travel to the cloud. Offlyn.ai helps teams reduce cost, latency, bandwidth usage, privacy exposure, carbon intensity, and datacenter water demand while preserving cloud access for tasks that truly require larger models.

---

## AI Resource Efficiency and SCI-AI GreenOps

Most AI tools optimize only for output quality. Offlyn optimizes for quality, cost, privacy, carbon, water, and resilience.

Our open-source audits help teams estimate:

- Cloud tokens avoided
- API dollars saved
- Cloud inference workload reduced
- Incremental local energy used
- Consumer SCI-AI operational proxy metrics
- Estimated cloud carbon emissions avoided
- Estimated datacenter cooling water avoided
- Network transfer and storage avoided
- Sensitive data kept local
- Quality preserved through hybrid routing

Offlyn's reporting model is SCI-AI-aligned and ISO/IEC 21031:2024-informed. It supports AI-native functional units such as gCO2e per meeting hour, gCO2e per second of audio processed, gCO2e per 1,000 tokens, and gCO2e per workflow execution.

These are modeled estimates for decision support and are not carbon credits, offsets, or certified emissions reductions.

---

## What we build

### AI cost and sustainability optimization

Tools, benchmarks, and audit frameworks that measure how much cloud AI resource use can be reduced through local-first inference, hybrid routing, and workload-aware architecture design.

### Local and edge AI

Offline-first apps and agent runtimes for laptops, phones, edge devices, and disconnected environments.

### Hardware-aware AI

Experiments that connect AI software decisions to silicon, memory, quantization, energy use, and accelerator design.

### Verification and resilience

Audit layers, policy logs, claims frameworks, and benchmark traces that help teams understand when, where, and why AI workloads were routed locally, to the edge, or to the cloud.

---

## Featured repositories

| Repository | Focus |
|------------|-------|
| [offlyn-token-savings-audit](https://github.com/offlyn-ai/offlyn-token-savings-audit) | Token, cost, carbon, water, privacy, and SCI-AI reporting for cloud-first, local-first, and hybrid AI workflows. |
| [silicafold-offlyn.ai-chip](https://github.com/offlyn-ai/silicafold-offlyn.ai-chip) | Open-silicon Tiny Tapeout project exploring folded INT4 TensorTile and PolicyGate primitives for offline SLM-agent research. |
| [offlyn-clipper](https://github.com/offlyn-ai/offlyn-clipper) | Offline-first personal work intelligence layer for local transcription, search, and private knowledge workflows. |

---

## Core thesis

AI cost and sustainability are not only model problems.

They are full-stack optimization problems across prompts, context windows, retrieval, routing, caching, quantization, local inference, edge deployment, hardware acceleration, observability, privacy, carbon intensity, water efficiency, and compliance.

Offlyn.ai exists to make that optimization visible, measurable, and deployable.
